evidence.evidence_text: iary activators than in Ca(OH) 2-activated GGBS without auxiliary activators and at water curing than at air-dried curing. In addition, the compressive strength of Ca(OH) 2-activated GGBS without auxiliary activators decreased at 91 days compared with 28-day strength. Thus, the growth and density of hydration prod- ucts were consistent with the strength results. The hydration products that were found easily in the SEM images were CSH gels and C 2ASH8 hydrates coagulated around the GGBS particles [10]. The molar Si/Ca ratio in the CSH gels gen- erally increased with the increase of hydration time, showing a higher Si/Ca ratio in water curing than in air

increases [6]. The CSH gels with a higher Si/Ca ratio seem to be favorable for enhancing the strength devel- opment of AA GGBS [6,10]. 4.3. Compressive strength at 28 days The effect of the W/B ratio and S/B ratio on the 28-day compres- sive strength (f 0 c ) of AA GGBS mortars tested is shown in Fig. 7. Sim- ilarly to the case of OPC concrete, f 0 c of Ca(OH) 2-based GGBS mortars decreased with the increase of W/B ratio, showing a simi- lar decreasing rate regardless of the type of auxiliary activators and curing condition. At the same W/B ratio, Ca(OH)2 and Na2SiO3-acti- vated GGBS mortars developed a higher f 0 c than Ca(OH) 2 and Na2CO3-activated G

4.4. Compressive strength development The compressive strength of air-cured pastes activated by the single Ca(OH)2 reduced after 56 days by an average of 15% of their own 28-day strength, whereas that of the Ca(OH) 2-based activated pastes with the auxiliary activators improved at a long-term age, as given in Table 2 . A valid addition of auxiliary activators can stabi- lize the hydration reactivity of Ca(OH) 2-activated GGBS pastes at a long-term age [6,10]. OPC concrete. On the other hand, the values for A1 and B1 are minimally affected by the S/B ratio, as given in Table 4 . 5. Predictions of compressive strength development 5.1. Simple equations For a further development for precast products and/or mixing design of Ca(OH) 2-based AA GGBS concrete, simple equations to predict f 0 c and the two parameters A1 and B1 in Eq. (1) were pro- posed based on nonlinear multiple regression (NLMR) analysis of test data. review_queue.question: Packet contains compressive-strength plots or image-only tables, but exact numeric mixture-level values are not reliably readable.
review_queue.suggested_action: Digitize the compressive-strength figure/table from the source PDF and map values to mixture IDs, ages, and MPa units.

main_risks: compressive-strength values are present only in figures/image tables and require digitization
